- Updated report header template with new date+time format

feat: Shardlake v0 — decoupled vector search prototype (#3)

* Initial plan

* feat: implement Shardlake full workspace prototype

Replaces the minimal scaffold with a complete 7-crate Rust workspace:

- shardlake-core: domain types, errors, SystemConfig
- shardlake-storage: ObjectStore trait + LocalObjectStore backend
- shardlake-manifest: versioned manifest schema + alias lifecycle
- shardlake-index: K-means IndexBuilder (BuildParams), binary ShardIndex
  format, exact search, merge-top-k, recall@k, lazy IndexSearcher
- shardlake-serve: axum 0.8 HTTP API (/health, /query)
- shardlake-bench: recall@k + latency benchmark harness
- shardlake-cli: shardlake binary (ingest, build-index, publish, serve, benchmark)

Also adds:
- fixtures/sample_10.jsonl: 10-vector 2D fixture
- tools/gen_sample.py: random JSONL generator
- config/default.toml: default SystemConfig
- Makefile with demo target (ingest → build-index → publish → benchmark)
- README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, DECISIONS.md, ROADMAP.md

All 12 tests pass; cargo fmt + clippy -D warnings clean.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add docs/ folder with user-facing documentation and update AGENTS.md

feat: show fallback warning on OLED when no v2 state exists

Before the first valid v2 MQTT message arrives, the OLED now displays
a centered warning: 'No OLED state / Awaiting MQTT...' and logs a
warning (once). When a valid v2 message arrives, the fallback clears
and normal v2 rendering begins.

On invalid v2 payloads, shows 'OLED state error / Bad MQTT payload'
on the display before re-raising the ValueError.

docs: comprehensive documentation audit (#1124)

## Summary

Complete documentation audit covering schema gaps, stub expansions, and
accuracy fixes across the entire `docs/` directory.

### New Content
- New `docs/office365-integration.md` (299 lines) — Azure app
registration, OAuth PKCE flow, platform config, troubleshooting
- `SUMMARY.md` updated with Office 365 entry

### Schema Gap Fills
- **`apps.md`**: 15+ missing fields (type, autoStart, skills,
skillSettings, iassistant, imageGeneration, thinking,
upload/settings/starterPrompts sub-fields)
- **`models.md`**: 14 missing fields + `iassistant-conversation`
provider, rebuilt property table
- **`platform.md`**: `auth.mode` fixed (added ldap/ntlm), 9 new sections
(rateLimit, ssl, logging, jwt, iFinder, iAssistant, oauth, cloudStorage,
skills)

### Expanded Stub Docs
- `styles.md`: 16 → 77 lines (all 15 built-in styles, runtime flow)
- `prompts.md`: 17 → 173 lines (fixed incorrect `prompts.json` claim —
they're individual files)
- `microphone-feature.md`: 28 → 130 lines (14 languages, voice commands,
Azure Speech config)
- `magic-prompt-feature.md`: 31 → 88 lines (API spec, 3-level fallback
chain)
- `ui.md`: 281 → 431 lines (theme, PWA, app categories sections)

### Architecture & Security Docs
- **`architecture.md`**: Workflow System + Skills System sections,
corrected file paths, updated client feature list
- **`external-authentication.md`**: LDAP and NTLM sections,
`workflows`/`skills` permissions
- **`configuration-validation.md`**: 5 new schemas (workflow, catalog,
registry, mimetype, cloudStorage)
- **`security.md`**: "Secret Encryption at Rest" section (AES-256-GCM,
encrypted fields table, guard pattern)
- **`tool-calling.md`**: Built-in tools reference (7 tools),
`isSpecialTool` concept; `tools.md` → redirect stub

### Accuracy Fixes
- `jwt-authentication.md`: cookie-first token extraction documented
- `oidc-authentication.md`: RS256 is the default (not HS256); fix
JWT_SECRET guidance
- `production-reverse-proxy-guide.md`: knownRoutes updated from 5 → 12
actual routes
- `platform.md`: removed misleading `DATABASE_URL` example
- `custom-renderers.md`: all examples now use `UserComponent` naming
convention
- `jwt-well-known-endpoints.md` + `ihub-as-oidc-idp.md`:
`claims_supported` corrected to all 9 claims
- `oauth-authorization-code.md`: `POST /api/oauth/introspect` endpoint
documented (RFC 7662)
- `server-config.md`: 14+ missing env vars added (JWT keys, Azure, NTLM,
BASE_PATH, SSL, etc.)

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify docs render correctly in the documentation site
- [ ] Spot-check `apps.md` schema fields against
`server/validators/appConfigSchema.js`
- [ ] Spot-check `platform.md` sections against
`server/defaults/config/platform.json`
- [ ] Verify Office 365 Azure setup steps match current
`Office365Service.js` OAuth flow

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- PRM policy dynamically builds resource URL from the path parameter

commit.message:
Sync with copilot-sdk: add onEvent to SessionConfig and ResumeSessionConfig (#43)

- Add optional onEvent Closure property to SessionConfig
- Add optional onEvent Closure property to ResumeSessionConfig
- Apply onEvent handler in Client::createSession() after session registration
- Apply onEvent handler in Client::resumeSession() after session registration
- Update submodule pointer to 27f487f
- Update docs/jp/session-config.md with onEvent documentation

The onEvent handler is registered before the session.create/resume RPC is issued
in the official SDK so that early events (e.g. session.start) are not missed.

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: replace `getConfigValue` with direct `getConfig()` property access (#3589)

`getConfigValue(path: string)` accepted arbitrary string paths and
returned `any`, making typos silently return `undefined` at runtime and
forcing callers to add unverified type annotations or casts.

## Changes

- **Deleted `getConfigValue`** from `get-config.ts` along with the
now-unused `lodash/get` import
- **Migrated all ~20 call sites** to direct `getConfig()` property
access, removing manual type annotations and casts that only existed to
compensate for the `any` return type:

```ts
// before
const docsLink = getConfigValue('monitor.docsLink');  // any; typo = silent undefined
const schemas = getConfigValue('pathAgnosticDecorations') as TPathAgnosticDecorationSchema[] | undefined;

// after
const docsLink = getConfig().monitor?.docsLink;        // string | undefined, compiler-checked
const schemas = getConfig().pathAgnosticDecorations;   // readonly TPathAgnosticDecorationSchema[] | undefined
```

- **Type fixes surfaced by the migration:**
- `link-patterns.ts`: replaced `.filter(Boolean)` with a type predicate
to properly narrow `ProcessedLinkPattern[]`
- `transform-trace-data.ts`: cast `readonly string[]` → `string[]` for
`orderTags` parameter
- `TopNav.tsx`: added `?? ''` fallback for `qualityMetrics.menuLabel`
(`string | undefined`)
- `DependencyGraph/index.tsx`: replaced `||` with `??` for
`dagMaxNumServices` fallback

- **Updated all test files** that mocked `getConfigValue` to mock the
`getConfig` default export instead, passing structured config objects
rather than scalar values

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>[chore]: `getConfigValue` provides no type safety for
config access</issue_title>
> <issue_description>## Problem
> 
> `getConfigValue` accepts an arbitrary string path and returns `any`:
> 
> ```typescript
> // get-config.ts
> export function getConfigValue(path: string) {
>   return _get(getConfig(), path);
> }
> ```
> 
> This means two classes of error go undetected at compile time:
> 
> **1. Mistyped or renamed paths are silently undefined at runtime**
> 
> ```typescript
> getConfigValue('monitor.menuEnable') // typo — returns undefined, no
error
> getConfigValue('storagecapabilities.metricsStorage') // wrong case —
returns undefined
> ```
> 
> A recent bug in this codebase was caused by exactly this:
`storageCapabilities.metricsStorage`
> was accessed through `getConfigValue` and the result was used as a
boolean gate, but the path
> was wrong in an earlier version of the code and silently returned
`undefined` (falsy), causing
> the Monitor page to always show the empty state.
> 
> **2. Return types are `any`, so callers must annotate manually or
cast**
> 
> ```typescript
> // Caller must add an explicit annotation just to get type safety:
> const config: MonitorEmptyStateConfig =
getConfigValue('monitor.emptyState');
> 
> // Or cast, which defeats the purpose entirely:
> const schemas = getConfigValue('pathAgnosticDecorations') as
TPathAgnosticDecorationSchema[] | undefined;
> ```
> 
> These manual annotations are not checked against the actual type at
that path in `Config` —
> they are just asserted. A refactor that changes the `Config` type will
not flag these call sites.
> 
> **Scope:** `getConfigValue` is called in ~20 places across the
codebase. `getConfig()` is used
> directly in a handful of places and is already safe by construction
(TypeScript checks property
> access against the `Config` type).
> 
> ## Proposal — Replace `getConfigValue` calls with direct `getConfig()`
access
> 
> For all call sites where the path is a fixed, known string, replace:
> 
> ```typescript
> // before
> const docsLink = getConfigValue('monitor.docsLink');
> const enabled: boolean =
getConfigValue('storageCapabilities.metricsStorage');
> ```
> 
> with direct property access:
> 
> ```typescript
> // after
> const docsLink = getConfig().monitor.docsLink; // type: string |
undefined
> const enabled = getConfig().storageCapabilities.metricsStorage; //
type: boolean
> ```
> 
> This requires no new infrastructure and is already how
`tracking/index.ts` and
> `TraceTimelineViewer/duck.ts` access config. The memoization in
`getConfig()` means there is
> no performance difference.
> 
> ## Suggested migration order
> 
> 1. Switch all `getConfigValue` call sites to direct `getConfig()`
property access, removing
> any manual type annotations or casts that were only needed to
compensate for the `any`
> return type. Affected files: `TopNav.tsx`,
`Monitor/ServicesView/index.tsx`,
> `Monitor/EmptyState/index.tsx`, `ThemeStorage.ts`,
`DependencyGraph/index.tsx`,
> `link-patterns.ts`, `path-agnostic-decorations.ts`,
`DeepDependencies/index.tsx`,
> `DeepDependencies/SidePanel/index.tsx`, `SearchForm.tsx`,
`SearchResults/AltViewOptions.tsx`,
> `TraceId.tsx`, `TraceDiffGraph.tsx`, `transform-trace-data.ts`,
`get-target.ts`,
>    `process-scripts.ts`.
> 2. Delete `getConfigValue` from `get-config.ts` and remove its export
from any index files.
> 3. Delete the `getConfigValue` tests, or convert them to test
`getConfig()` directly.
> </issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

- Fixes jaegertracing/jaeger-ui#3588

Signed-off-by: Yuri Shkuro <github@ysh.us>
Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: yurishkuro <3523016+yurishkuro@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Yuri Shkuro <github@ysh.us>

[MEN-108] WS2: Domain & Database — Flyway V1-V9, JPA Entities, Repositories (#1)

* feat(domain/db): add Flyway migrations V1-V9

- V1: pgcrypto + uuid-ossp extensions
- V2: allergen + allergen_translation reference tables
- V3: user_account + user_allergen_profile (GDPR pseudonymization)
       + consent_record + oauth_account

commit.message:
refactor: remove OLED v1 backward compatibility

Remove the legacy MQTT message topic, v1 message handler, and associated
infrastructure now that the v2 contract is the sole OLED display interface.

Removed:
- MQTT topic 'smartchime/display/message' subscription and config
- handle_message() and set_scrolling_message() methods
- update_motion_status() method (motion state now via v2 contract)
- self.motion_active / self.last_motion_time instance vars
- TestHandleMessage, TestV2BackwardCompatibility test classes
- v1-specific test cases (set_scrolling_message, update_motion_status)

Kept (used by AirPlay internal overlay):
- set_temporary_message() / clear_temporary_message()
- _restore_original_message() — updated to use _apply_v2_content()
  when v2 state is active

commit.message:
Fix tonearm not resetting on track loop

The tonearm read audioPosition from the Zustand store, which was only
set once at load time and never updated during playback. When a track
looped, the stale position stayed at the end, making the tonearm start
outside the record.

Now reads the LIVE position from DeckAudioPlayer.getPosition(), which
wraps correctly via modulo when the track loops. Also fixed the platter
rotation angle which had the same stale-position issue.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add crictl example for bare Linux usage (#13)

Step-by-step guide to running VM-isolated containers on a bare Linux
machine using crictl — no Kubernetes required.

  shim, kernel, rootfs) and container lifecycle with crictl

  curls the endpoint, and shows timing

Tells the story: pull image → create pod (boot VM) → create container
(hot-plug disk) → start → curl → see response from inside a microVM.

commit.message:
Skip INTL0003 PascalCase validation for test methods (#374)

INTL0003 was flagging test methods with underscores (e.g.,
`Method_Scenario_ExpectedResult`) as PascalCase violations. Test methods
commonly use underscores for readability and should be exempt.

**Changes:**
- Added `IsTestMethod()` check in `NamingMethodPascal` analyzer to skip
methods decorated with test attributes
- Uses namespace-based detection to automatically recognize test
attributes from xUnit, NUnit, MSTest, and TUnit frameworks
- Any attribute from the `Xunit`, `NUnit.Framework`,
`Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting`, or `TUnit.Core`
namespaces is automatically recognized
- Includes fallback to common test attribute name checking for test
environments where namespace metadata may be incomplete
- Added test coverage for MSTest, xUnit, and NUnit frameworks
- Updated docs to document the exception

**Example:**
```csharp
[TestClass]
public class FooTests
{
    [TestMethod]
    public void FooThing_IsFooThing_HasFooThing() // No longer triggers INTL0003
    {
        Assert.IsTrue(true);
    }
    
    public void myNonTestMethod() // Still triggers INTL0003
    {
    }
}
```

This namespace-based approach provides better maintainability and
automatically supports new test attributes added to these frameworks
without requiring code changes to the analyzer.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Test Method triggers INTL0003</issue_title>
> <issue_description>A Test method like
"FooThing_IsFooThing_HasFooThing" Triggers INTL0003 in a test project
when I don't think it should</issue_description>
> 
> <agent_instructions>FIRST write a test that fails to prove the bug.
THEN write a fix, to fix the bug and make sure the test

Consolidate master FX: add individual effect browser + 25 new presets

- DJFxQuickPresets now has 'Add Individual Effect' section showing all 49
  effects from the unified registry, grouped by category (Dynamics,
  Distortion, Reverb & Delay, Modulation, Filter, etc.)
- Added 25 new factory presets using previously missing effects:
  DJ: Dub Echo, Filter Sweep, Tape Stop, Lo-Fi Radio, Phaser Wash,
      Auto-Pan, Vibrato Wobble, Frequency Shift, Feedback Loop,
      Stone Phaser, Vox Amp Crunch, WAM Delay Wash, Pitch Up, GEQ Scoop
  Genre: Psychedelic, Synthwave, Shoegaze, Acid House, Garage/2-Step
  Wide: Leslie Cabinet, Space Echo, Hall Reverb
  Warm: Tube Screamer Glow, Rotary Warmth
  Loud: Big Muff Wall, QuadraFuzz Smash, Disto Machine
  Vinyl: Cassette Deck
- Added Neural category to DJ preset dropdown
- Effect types now covered in presets: 43 of 49 (6 niche effects
  still available via individual effect browser)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve Salt 3007 compatibility issues

- Remove 'depth' arg from git.cloned (not supported in Salt 3007)
- Fix malformed github-cli.list: put deb URI on single line
- Replace onfail_stop (invalid keyword) in editors state with
  'unless: ! which code' so extensions skip gracefully when VSCode
  is not yet connected via WSL extension
- Fix Salt bootstrap URL (bootstrap.saltproject.io returns HTML;
  switch to proper Broadcom apt repo install)
